title: Support having both Kubernetes YAML file and Containerfile at the same level
body:
### Feature request description

I've got a single-project repo with the project's source at its very root. I also have a Containerfile at the same level, which does `COPY . .` to build the project in the container. Sadly, this means I cannot write a working kube file that would run this Containerfile to create an image. To use this feature I'd need to specifically name a directory as the name of the image — I cannot use neither `.` (or `./`) nor tricks like `src/../`. 

I'm talking about this feature ([from the docs](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-kube-play.1.html)):

> ```
> apiVersion: v1
> kind: Pod
> metadata:
> ...
> spec:
>   containers:
>   - name: container
>     image: foobar
> ...
> ```
> If there is a directory named foobar in the current working directory with a file named Containerfile or Dockerfile, Podman kube play builds that image and name it foobar. An example directory structure for this example looks like:
> ```
> |- mykubefiles
>     |- myplayfile.yaml
>     |- foobar
>          |- Containerfile
> ```

My project structure is more like this:
```
|- myproject
    |- myplayfile.yaml
    |- Containerfile
    |- src/
    |- (other source files)
```

### Suggest potential solution

Support using `.` (or `./`) as the image name to indicate that the current directory should be used to lookup the necessary Containerfile.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

To build the image, I'd need to put Containerfile in a subdirectory — which breaks it because the `COPY ` directive ignores navigation to parent directories `../`, so I cannot use `COPY ../ .` to copy my source code into the container from outside of the Containerfile's subdirectory.

I also tried placing a symlink to the parent directory into the Containerfile's subdirectory (e.g. `src` linked to `../`) and writing `COPY src/ .`, but of course it didn't follow the symlink and just copied it.

One workaround is to go back to the regular project structure (with both the kube file and the Containerfile at the root), specify an empty and gitignored subdirectory (e.g. `api-container`) in the kube file as the image name, and use `rsync -r . api-container` each time you make a change to sync your repository's code into that subdirectory. Then you can just run `podman kube play myplayfile.yaml`.

Another workaround (learned from reading #17278) is to specify the project's root directory name (e.g. `api`) as the image name, then go one directory up (`cd ../`) and run the Pod definition file from there (like `podman kube play api/myplayfile.yaml`). Of course, the project's root directory name is not guaranteed to be the same for everyone, so this workaround is slightly worse (although easier).

### Additional context

The error message if you try to specify `.` as the image name:
```
Error: parsing reference ".": invalid reference format
```

comments.body:
> Support using . (or ./) as the image name to indicate that the current directory should be used to lookup the necessary Containerfile.

How would the code then know what the image name should be? The reason the directory name is used so it can generate a stable image name of it.

Good point. One option could be to use the name of the cwd, but I guess that's too variable, so it doesn't count.

Now that you pointed this out, I've come up with a better (and more obvious, now that I think about it) workaround — just build the container image separately before running the kube file.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
